Form: FNM1073
Purpose: Purchase
Address: 2 Andy Ct, Bloomington, IL
Year Built: 1998
Appraised Value: $150,000

Highest and Best Use criteria: The reasonably probable and legal use of vacant land or an improved property that is physically possible, appropriately supported, financially feasible, and that results in the highest value. The four criteria the highest and best use must meet are legal permissibility, physical possibility, financial feasibility, and maximum productivity.  Extra Comments ANSI Standards Gross living area calculations are derived from the measurement of the subject property and reported on the dimension and sketch pages of this report. Depreciation is estimated by the appraiser observations. The appraiser has adhered to ANSI standards in measuring the subject property.  Clarification of Intended Use and Intended User: First State Mortgage  The Intended User of this appraisal report is the Lender/Client. The Intended Use is to evaluate the property that is the subject of this appraisal for a mortgage finance transaction, subject to the stated Scope of Work, purpose of the appraisal, reporting requirements of this appraisal report form, and Definition of Market Value. No additional Intended Users are identified by the appraiser.  Effective January 1st, 2010, the ETHICS RULE now requires an appraiser to disclose any prior services provided regarding the subject property within the three years preceding the new request / assignment. The appraiser has not appraised the property in the past three years preceeding this request.   This appraisal report adheres to the development and reporting options of The Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice (USPAP), FIRREA Title XI Appraisal Regulations and The Appraisal Independence Standards set forth by H.R 4173.  Geographical Competency Statement: This appraiser has geographic competency. The appraiser has prepared the appraisal in a familiar location and has spent sufficient time to understand the nuances of the local market and the supply and demand factors relating to the specific property type and the location involved.  Section 1455.245  Scope of Property Condition Inspections by Real Estate Appraisers   Licensed real estate appraisers may include in an appraisal report comments on the condition of the property that affect property value, including physical deficiencies, adverse conditions and renovations, based on observations by the licensed real estate appraiser during the appraisal of the property. Licensed real estate appraisers may not provide these comments for compensation, or with the expectation of receiving compensation, directly or indirectly, as a substitute for a home inspection, or a home inspection report, prepared by a licensed home inspector. All appraisal reports of a property that is 1 to 4 residential units prepared by licensed real estate appraisers must include the following statement: "The comments by the licensed real estate appraiser contained within this appraisal report on the condition of the property do not address "standards of practice" as defined in the Home Inspector License Act [225 ILCS 441] and 68 Ill. Adm. Code 1410 and are not to be considered a home inspection or home inspection report."  ILLINOIS FEE DISCLOSURE: To comply with Illinois state regulations, when an AMC engages an appraiser for an assignment, the appraiser must prominently display the appraisal fee received from the AMC in the certification as follows:   The compensation for this appraisal assignment is $450.00.  The appraiser has adhered to ANSI standards in measuring the subject property.   Underwriter Conditions  Please update and comment that this property consists of on 4 units in the HOA. And also update the number of units, units sold, etc  - Based on the information provided by the subject's HOA, the subject phase section has been updated to reflect the four units included in the subject's HOA. A comment has been made in the management group section that the HOA consists of four units.   Sorry, forgot to request that the appraiser update the HOA dues  - Based on the information provided by the subject's HOA, the monthly HOA has been updated to $100/month.   APPRAISER TO PLEASE CORRECT PROJECT INFORMATION TO MATCH PHASE INFO  - Project information has been updated to match the subject phase information. Sorry for the misunderstanding and inconvenience.

Form: FNM1004
Purpose: Purchase
Address: 213 E Charles St, Mc Lean, IL
Year Built: 1973
Appraised Value: $210,000

Due to a lack of recent comparable sales within McLean, IL, the search was expanded to include other competing rural communities. Additionally, it was necessary to analyze sales occurring up to 18 months prior to the effective date to identify sales that reflect market support for the premium paid for dwellings of similar GLA, quality, and condition within the subject's market area. The comparables were selected to demonstrate the marketability of houses with a similar location, design, age, and condition as the subject. Due to recent market trends the comparables were applied with a market time adjustment of 7.0% based on the sales statistics chart included in this report. The subject property is of superior quality and condition relative to typical ranch-style dwellings in the McLean, IL market area. Market participants seeking ranch homes in this area demonstrate a willingness to pay a premium for properties that reflect similar quality and condition. The subject's finished basement is of above-average quality and contributes additional value, as this level of finish is supported by comparable sales and recognized by buyers in the market. Comparable #1 has been adjusted for its site size, quality, age, bathroom count, GLA, lack of a basement, and lack of a shed. Comparable #2 has been adjusted for its site size, view, quality, age, bathroom count, and deck count. Comparable #3 has been adjusted for its site size, view, bathroom count, basement finished room count, deck, patio, and lack of a fence. Comparable #4 has been adjusted for its site size, quality, bathroom count, GLA, lack of a basement, and garage space count. Comparable #5 has been adjusted for its site size, quality, age, bathroom count, GLA, basement finished room count, lack of a fence, and lack of a shed. Comparable #6 has been adjusted for its sales concessions, site size, view, quality, age, GLA, and basement finished room count. Comparable #7 has been included to bracket the subject's site size and bedroom count. It has been adjusted for its site size, quality, condition, garage space count, and lack of a shed. After adjustments are made the comparables provide a good basis for an estimate of value.
